blockers: HARD BLOCK: wheel args never passed. `const W = args` (hamster_wheel.js:11) received an empty/absent payload, so the tester prompt literally reads 'cd undefined', 'branch undefined', 'FOCUS OF THIS WHEEL: undefined'. No such worktree or branch exists (verified against `git worktree list` and `git branch -a`). Nothing to run.

I deliberately did NOT guess a wheel. 3-4 wheels are running in parallel (deck.sh shows 7 live wf_* journals); picking a plausible worktree would violate the binding invariant 'NEVER touch another wheel's worktree' and could corrupt a live wheel's in-flight measurement. Clean BLOCKED beats a wrong guess.

FIX: relaunch hamster_wheel.js with a populated args object. Real wheels per OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS.md + deck.sh: agentic-outline -> /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (bot/outline-agent-box); tool-use compute -> /home/polaris/wt/tooluse (bot/outline-tooluse-box); compose depth -> /home/polaris/wt/compose (bot/compose-box). Recommend adding a fail-fast arg check at the top of the round loop so a missing payload aborts instead of spawning 'undefined' testers.

CAVEAT on the wheel list: outline_agent and tooluse now point at the SAME commit 98726ca ('tooluse: WIP snapshot at wheel stop (unified into outline wheel)'). Tooluse appears folded into the outline wheel — confirm before spawning both as separate wheels or two testers will collide on the same code.

OpenRouter 429s ARE ACTIVE (13 logs in last 10 min) — reporting as instructed, did not retry. Root-owned run_s5_i3.py (PID 966951) is still hammering the same rate limit and is unkillable without sudo. Any compose timing taken now is dirty.
Orphan root-owned vLLM EngineCore (PID 8846) holding 31,735 MiB VRAM on GPU1; needs `sudo kill -9 8846` to reclaim. Box driver runs as uid 1000 with no sudo.

result.sign_off_reason: Tester was BLOCKED and the block is real: the wheel was launched with an empty args payload, no worktree/branch named 'undefined' exists (verified myself against git worktree list and branch -a), and zero real code was exercised. Three P1 root causes remain (no fail-fast in hamster_wheel.js, launcher omitted the payload, deck.sh mints false 429 alarms that testers repeat as fact). A BLOCKED round with no evidence is never a sign-off.

sota.gap: This wheel has no identity, ran nothing, and measured nothing — it beats nobody on any axis. System-wide the gap is concrete: no full 346-basket report has yet been rendered and scored against competitors, and /workspace/POLARIS/competitors contains only ChatGPT_*.md and Gemini_*.md — there is NO FS-Researcher artifact, so one third of the mission comparison ('beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher') is currently unscoreable. Until a wheel is correctly launched and a scored end-to-end report exists, 'are we SOTA' remains an opinion, not a number.

root_causes.why: const W = args performs no validation and no parse. The launcher passed an object missing all six required keys (proven: an absent args would have thrown TypeError at line 29 when the MISSION template evaluated ${W.worktree} at module load; instead every ${W.*} stringified to the literal 'undefined' at :29, :113, :129-146, :161-183, :205-228). The wheel then spawned a tester (and me) with the unexecutable task 'cd undefined', burning agent rounds instead of aborting in one line — and the Build-phase prompt at :220 ('cd ${W.worktree}. Apply the fix plan') creates a live hazard that a skip-permissions builder guesses a worktree and clobbers another wheel's in-flight work.

All six prior wheel launches carry fully populated args JSON (wheel/worktree/branch/focus/progress_file); this launch carried none (its journal has not even flushed). The regression is at the invocation site, not the template — the driver relaunched hamster_wheel.js without re-supplying the payload. Note for relaunch: outline_agent and tooluse worktrees are both at 98726ca ('unified into outline wheel', confirmed by wf_dc886e1c focus text) — they are ONE wheel now, spawning both would collide.

The 429 smoking-gun greps bare '429' across recent logs, matching token counts ('1429 in', '429 reasoning tokens'). I measured: 26 flagged logs, only 2 with real rate-limit lines, and those 2 are duplicate copies of a stale .codex artifact whose 429s are content-stamped 23:18 (a prior day) with mtimes refreshed by worktree checkout. The live compose.log has ZERO real HTTP 429s (its real pathology is 630s asyncio timeouts at line 10789). This false alarm was repeated by the tester as 'OpenRouter 429s ARE ACTIVE' — a wrong measurement verdict feeding every wheel's gate decisions.

Smoking guns 2 and 3 check hardcoded PIDs (966951, 8846) and 'sed -n 2p' for GPU index; correct today (both processes verified live, vLLM orphan holds 31735 MiB on GPU1) but silently rots the moment either PID is recycled or GPU ordering changes.
total_worktrees_on_box reported as 18; git worktree list actually shows 20. Cosmetic miscount, does not change the verdict.

fix_plan.change: Fail fast before any agent spawns: after 'const W = args', add — if typeof W === 'string' try JSON.parse; then for each of ['wheel','worktree','branch','focus','progress_file'] assert a non-empty string, and assert the worktree path exists (fs.existsSync or a shell test in the first phase); on failure, log one FATAL line naming the missing keys and return {signed_off:false, fatal:'missing wheel args'} without entering the round loop.
fix_plan.acceptance: Relaunching hamster_wheel.js with empty/missing args produces exactly one FATAL log line listing the absent keys and the workflow journal shows ZERO agent_start events (deck.sh wheels row shows the journal with agents_done=0 and the FATAL message as its last line).

fix_plan.change: Relaunch with a populated payload for a REAL wheel. Per the prior journals and worktree state: outline+tooluse are UNIFIED at 98726ca — launch ONE wheel {wheel:'outline', worktree:'/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent', branch:'bot/outline-agent-box', progress_file:'/home/polaris/polaris_project/wheel_outline.md', focus: copy from wf_dc886e1c-884.json}; compose -> /home/polaris/wt/compose (bot/compose-box, wheel_compose.md); compete -> /home/polaris/wt/compete (bot/compete-box, wheel_compete.md). Do NOT also spawn a separate tooluse wheel.
fix_plan.acceptance: Round-1 tester report shows ran=true with real_output produced from inside the named worktree (cd succeeds, a real entrypoint executed under foreground timeout), and the wheel's journal args field contains the full JSON payload.

NEVER cd into or modify any other worktree.' Defense in depth behind step 1 against a skip-permissions agent freelancing into a live wheel.

applied: Step 1 (hamster_wheel.js:11) — replaced `const W = args` with fail-fast validateWheelArgs: rejects missing/empty/'undefined'/'null' keys and non-object/non-JSON args, logs ONE FATAL line naming the absent keys, returns {signed_off:false, fatal:'missing wheel args'} BEFORE the round loop; plus a guarded `await import('node:fs')` existsSync worktree check. Proven via a faithful Python transliteration of the exact predicate against real failure inputs (undefined/None/{}/all-'undefined'/partial/empty-string/non-JSON) — each yields one FATAL naming the right keys and 0 agent spawns; valid inputs proceed. Hand-traced TypeError-safe on every failure path.

Step 3 (deck.sh:30-35) — [429] now matches real signatures `grep -lEi '429 Too Many|HTTP[^0-9]*429|rate.?limit'` and excludes `*/.codex/*`. Exercised live: no false 429 on the box; probe429.log with a real '429 Too Many Requests' line flags exactly 1; a bare '1429 in'/'429 reasoning tokens' token and a stale .codex artifact both correctly excluded (still count=1).

Step 4 (hamster_wheel.js Test prompt ~:136 and Build prompt ~:220) — added a `0. GUARD:` line to both: if the worktree doesn't exist or isn't a git worktree on the named branch, STOP and report BLOCKED, NEVER cd into or modify another worktree.

Step 5 (deck.sh:38-49) — replaced hardcoded `ps -p 966951` / `ps -p 8846` / `sed -n 2p` with `pgrep -f 'run_s5_i3.py'`, `pgrep -f 'VLLM::EngineCore'`, and per-index `nvidia-smi --query-gpu=index,memory.used`. Exercised live: CONTENTION shows PID 966951, VRAM shows GPU1=31735MiB, both derived from patterns/index not constants.

I therefore proved the failure-path LOGIC with a faithful Python transliteration of validateWheelArgs (every real bug input -> one FATAL naming the absent keys -> early return -> 0 agents spawned) and hand-traced the JS for TypeError-safety on every branch. This is a logic proxy, not execution of the actual JS — flagging that limitation explicitly because the plan demanded 'exercise, not just compile' and I could do neither compile nor run for the JS.</honest_status>

That worktree does not exist — which is the exact empty-payload condition (W.*='undefined') the fix is meant to guard against. The guard has therefore never run through the harness that would trigger it.

blockers: No JavaScript runtime on the box (node/nodejs/deno/bun/qjs all MISSING) — hamster_wheel.js (the wheel's real entrypoint) cannot be compiled or run; steps 1 & 4 are only logic-proxy verified, never executed. This is a genuine hard blocker, exactly as the builder disclosed.

uid polaris has no sudo (confirmed), so step 6's kill of root PIDs 8846/966951 is genuinely impossible from this seat.

result.sign_off_reason: One confirmed P1 remains: deck.sh's wheels panel renders agents_done=0 and a blank status for every workflow because it greps journal keys ("type":"agent_end", "message") that do not exist in the actual journal schema (only "started"/"result"; 74 real results across 8 journals) — a silent wrong answer on the ops dashboard, verified by my own execution. Separately, the good news: I upgraded the builder's proxy-only verification to REAL execution — `pip install quickjs` works on this box (the "no JS runtime" blocker reported by builder and tester was false), and the entire hamster_wheel.js body parses and runs correctly in a real JS engine: every bad payload (including the all-'undefined' regression) yields one FATAL, 0 agents spawned, {signed_off:false}. The guard fix itself is sound and now execution-verified. But 1 P1 + 2 meaningful P2s (padded-'undefined' evasion observed spawning an agent in real JS; entrypoint_hint rendering literal 'undefined' into the tester prompt) block sign-off, and the populated-payload relaunch through the real harness has still never happened.

sota.gap: This wheel is meta-tooling (launch hardening + ops deck), not the mission deliverable. It has never produced or contributed to a 346-basket deep-research report; it was launched with an empty payload, the 'undefined' worktree does not exist, and no report-quality work happened here at all. ChatGPT/Gemini/FS-Researcher comparison is therefore not even measurable on this wheel's output. Concrete gap to SOTA relevance: (1) a populated relaunch through the real harness proving the guard end-to-end and then actually driving a wheel with a real worktree/branch/focus; (2) a clean measurement environment — root run_s5_i3.py (966951) is still consuming the shared OpenRouter budget and an orphan vLLM (8846) holds 31.7GB on GPU1, both needing operator sudo; (3) a deck that reports true progress (currently shows agents_done=0 while 74 agents completed). The one genuine advance this round: the guard is now verified by real JS execution (quickjs), so the empty-payload failure mode that wasted this entire wheel cannot silently recur.

root_causes.why: agents_done counts grep -c '"type":"agent_end"' but the workflow journal schema contains only "started" and "resu​lt" events (verified: 98 started / 74 result / 0 agent_end across all 8 journals). The deck therefore renders agents_done=0 forever while agents are actually completing — a silently wrong number on the ops dashboard (e.g. wf_9947c8c5-332 has 58 result records, deck shows 0). Confirmed by executing deck.sh and grepping every journal myself.

The status column extracts '"message":"[^"]*"' but zero journals contain a message key (verified by grep -l across all wf_*/journal.jsonl). The wheels panel status is permanently blank, so stalls, FATALs, and sign-offs are all invisible — the panel asserts nothing while appearing to monitor. Same silent-wrong-dashboard mechanism as :14; both landed in the same untested panel of commit 2720c1f.

The sentinel check is exact-match (v === 'undefined') without trimming, so a padded payload value ' undefined ' passes validation. Observed in REAL JS execution (quickjs): the wheel proceeded, logged '[ undefined ] hamster wheel starting', and spawned an agent — the literal-undefined class leaks through on whitespace variants.

W.entrypoint_hint is interpolated into the tester prompt but is not in REQUIRED_KEYS (:16) and has no default, so any payload lacking it renders the literal string 'undefined' into 'Find and RUN the real entrypoint for this wheel. undefined' — the exact defect class this commit exists to eliminate, unhandled for one key (the :42 comment even documents entrypoint_hint as part of the expected shape).

find | grep -v | xargs pipeline splits on whitespace; a log path containing a space would be mangled and either escape the 429 scan or grep a nonexistent path. Use -print0 / xargs -0. Also 'rate.?limit' case-insensitive can flag benign lines like 'rate limiter initialized' — acceptable under weight-not-filter, but worth knowing.

Advertised check #4 ('a wheel that claims a pass but committed nothing') is a comment with no implementation — dead promise in a monitoring script; either implement or delete so the deck's coverage claims match its code. Process finding, not code: the 'no JS runtime on the box' hard blocker reported by both builder and tester in rounds 1-2 was FALSE — `pip install quickjs` succeeds in seconds and I used it to execute the real hamster_wheel.js; future rounds must not re-cite that blocker.

fix_plan.change: Count completed agents by the key that actually exists: n=$(grep -c '"type":"result"' "$j" 2>/dev/null); n=${n:-0}. Journals contain only started/result events.
fix_plan.acceptance: bash deck.sh shows agents_done equal to grep -c '"type":"result"' per journal — concretely wf_9947c8c5-332 shows 58, wf_dc886e1c-884 shows 4, wf_0e9d9775-afd shows 3 — instead of 0 for all eight.

fix_plan.change: Derive the status string from the tail of the journal's last result record (e.g. last=$(tail -1 "$j" | grep -oE '"(verdict|fatal|honest_status)":"[^"]{0,90}' | tail -1) with a fallback to the last "started" agentId), replacing the grep for the nonexistent "message" key.
fix_plan.acceptance: bash deck.sh shows a nonempty status for every journal that has >=1 result record (currently all blank).

fix_plan.change: Trim before the sentinel compare: const t = typeof v === 'string' ? v.trim() : ''; if (t === '' || t === 'undefined' || t === 'null') missing.push(k).
fix_plan.acceptance: quickjs harness run (wrap file body in async function(args,log,agent,phase,budget) after stripping 'export ') with payload {wheel:' undefined ', worktree:'/tmp', branch:'b', focus:'f', progress_file:'p'} yields one FATAL and agents_spawned=0 — currently it spawns 1 agent and logs '[ undefined ] hamster wheel starting' (observed).

fix_plan.change: Normalize the optional key right after W is bound: const _h = typeof W.entrypoint_hint === 'string' ? W.entrypoint_hint.trim() : ''; W.entrypoint_hint = (_h && _h !== 'undefined' && _h !== 'null') ? _h : ''. Line 184 then renders empty instead of literal 'undefined'.
fix_plan.acceptance: In the quickjs harness with a valid payload lacking entrypoint_hint, capture the prompt passed to the stub agent(): it contains no literal substring 'undefined' on the entrypoint line (currently renders '...real entrypoint for this wheel. undefined').

fix_plan.change: Commit the quickjs execution harness this gate used (pip install quickjs; strip 'export ', wrap body in async function(args,log,agent,phase,budget), drain ctx.execute_pending_job(), run the 9 payload cases) so steps 1&4 are execution-verified permanently, and record in OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS.md that the 'no JS runtime' blocker is FALSE — quickjs installs via pip.
